label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so today is September 9, 1909. I need to find out what the date is 24 hours later. Let's think about this step by step. First, 24 hours from any given date is just the same time the next day. So if today is September 9, then 24 hours later should be September 10, right? Unless there's some daylight saving time change or something, but in 1909, I don't think daylight saving time was even a thing yet. Most countries didn't implement it until much later, like during World War I. So no time changes to worry about here. So adding one day to September 9 would be September 10. The year is still 1909. So the date should be 09/10/1909. Let me check the options. Option A is 09/10/1909. That seems correct. Wait, but
